rdfs:comment.value: A pattern of an activity done within an experiment.
An execution of a specific activity described by an activity pattern. An activity execution takes place in a certain period of time.

An apperance of a participant. This is a concept for which subconcepts can be modelled to define so called user-defined model extension points. To define a model of apperance the subconcepts of this concept should be defined.

A placement of participants within group or two persons activities. This is a concept for which subconcepts can be modelled to define so called user-defined model extension points. To define a model of arrangement subconcepts of PersonalGroupArrangement of PersonalTwoPersonsArrangement concepts should be defined.
A medium for a registration of a signal holding informatin on observable symptoms.
Epoch time series with a constant epoch length.
A specific measure representing an emotional state.

A list of emotional state names. This is a concept for which subconcepts can be modelled to define so called user-defined model extension points. To define lexicons for emotion states names subconcepts of this concept should be defined.
Time series with data points referring to a whole epoch.
A list of activities done by participants in order to gather various biosignals and emotional states for the emotion recognition purpose.
A pattern of a group activity done within an experiment. This pattern describes an activity which is performed by three or more participants together.
An execution of a specific group activity described by a group activity pattern.
A pattern of an individual activity done within an experiment. This pattern describes an activity which is performed by one participant only.
An execution of a specific individual activity described by an individual activity pattern.
Time series with sampling done with a constant length time period.
Timestamp time series with sampling done with a constant length time period.
Conscious and unconscious actions of a human body, which generate a specified symptom of an emotional state, which can be further analysed in a process of emotion recognition.
Measured or estimated value which is stored within time series.

A measure name. This is a concept for which subconcepts can be modelled to define so called user-defined model extension points. To define lexicons for measure names subconcepts of this concept should be defined.
A type of observable information which is used as a proxy for the emotion recognition.
Observable information which is used as a proxy for the emotion recognition.
A person taking part in an experiment.
A state of a participant at a specific period of time.
A participation of a participant within a specific activity execution.
A placement of participants within group activities. To define a model of a group arrangement subconcepts of this concept should be defined.
A placement of participants within two persons activities. To define a model of a two persons arrangement subconcepts of this concept should be defined.

A personality of a participant. This is a concept for which subconcepts can be modelled to define so called user-defined model extension points. To define a personality model subconcepts of this concept should be defined.
A recording of a participation of a participant within an activity execution.
A specific channel recorded on registered data.
A registration of signals holding information on observable symptoms.
Time series with sampling done with a non-constant length time period.
Timestamp time series with sampling done with a non-constant length time period.
Holds information about a sex of participants.
Time series for a specific measure.
Time series with data points referring to a single moment of time.
A pattern of an activity done by two participants together within an experiment. This pattern describes an activity which is performed by exactly two participants together.
An execution of a specific two persons activity described by a two persons activity pattern.
Epoch time series with a non-constant epoch length.
A data property allowing to define a participant age for a specific period of time.
A data property allowing to define a date of birth for a participant.
A data property allowing to define a specific disorder of a participant.
A data property allowing to define a data type of a measure.
A data property allowing to define a range of a measure.
The datatype property, which allows for defiing unit of measure using The Unified Code for Units of Measure [https://ucum.org/trac].
A data property allowing to define a name of a specific entity.
An URI pointing at a recording data source. The URI address where recorded data is located.
An URI pointing at a time series source. The URI address where a file containing time series is located. The file is a csv file with a list of pairs consisting of a time stamp and a data point.
An object property allowing to define an activity pattern for an activity execution.
An object property allowing to define in which activity execution a participant takes part within a specific participation.
An object property allowing to define an apperance of a particpant in a specific time period.
An object property allowing to define an arrangement of participants within a specific activity execution.
An object property allowing to define a channel registered on a specific registered channel of registered data.
An object property allowing to define an observed life activity for a specific observable information.
An object property allowing to define a measure for a specific time series.
An object property allowing to define a measure name for a specific measure.
An object property allowing to define an observed modality for a specific observable information.
An object property allowing to define an observable information used to generate a specific time series.
An object property allowing to relate a participant state with a specific participant.
An object property allowing to define in what state a participant is in a specific participation.
An object property allowing to define which participation is recorded within a specific recording.
An object property allowing to define a personality of a participant within a specific period of time.
An object property allowing to define a recording for a specific observable information.
An object property allowing to define a registered channel for a specific recording.
An object property allowing to define registered data recorded on a specific registered channel.

An object property allowing to define scenarios of an experiment. A scenario is understood as a list of activity executions. The first activity execution within a scenario points at a consequent activity execution with the nextActivityExecution role.
An object property allowing to define a sex of a participant.
An inverse of the object property nextActivityExuction.
An inverse of the object property hasScenario.
An object property allowing to define a sequence of activities in a scenario.
Audio channel.
Blood Vlume Pulse (BVP) channel.
Chest size channel.
Depth video channel.
Electrocardiography channel.
Electrodermal activity channel.
Electroencephalography channel.
Electromyography channel.
RGB video channel.
Temperature channel.
Brain activity.
Heart activity.
Movement.
Muscle activity.
Perspiration.
Respiration.
Sound.
Thermal regulation.
Body posture.
Eye gaze.
Facial expressions.
Gestures.
Heart rate variability.
Head movement.
Heart rate.
Motion (different from motion identified by other individuals).
Muscle tension.
Neural activity.
Peripheral temperature.
Prosody of speech.
Rspiratory rate.
Skin conductance.
Vocalization.
Female.
Male.

notes: Pure ontology-oriented blueprint extracted from OWL.
No Mongo realization hints or derived implementation statuses are included.
Ontology header information and annotation literal metadata (lang/datatype) are preserved when available.
Subclass / equivalent / disjoint axioms are parsed directly from OWL XML.
Named individuals store asserted types directly; no separate class_assertion_axioms section is used.
RDF/XML schema extraction is merged with parser model output so schema-only and schema+individual files are both supported.
